articles.title: 2nd Mount Vernon police officer charged in 2 weeks raises alarm: "Who's doing the vetting?"
articles.author: Tony Aiello
articles.article_text: --- title: 2nd Mount Vernon police officer charged in 2 weeks raises alarm: "Who's doing the vetting?" author: Tony Aiello url: https://www.cbsnews.com/newyork/news/mount-vernon-police-officer-arrest-brandon-hunter-carney/ hostname: cbsnews.com description: A Mount Vernon, New York, police officer is on the wrong side of the law for the second time in two weeks. sitename: CBS New York date: 2026-04-09 categories: ['Local News'] tags: ['Mount Vernon, Westchester County'] --- Another Mount Vernon, New York, police officer is on the wrong side of the law, prosecutors said. It's the second time in two weeks a Mount Vernon police officer has been charged. 2 charged in 2 weeks Years before Brandon Hunter-Carney joined Mount Vernon Police, he allegedly took possession of a stolen check for $800,000, created a corporation using the same name, opened a bank account and deposited it. His arrest Wednesday came just days after Det. Kyren Braunskill was charged in an alleged on Long Island.Braunskill was hired by Mount Vernon shortly after he was fired as a New York State Prison Guard and put on a "decertification list" due to misconduct. He's accused of providing firearms to gang members while working as an NYPD 911 operator from 2021-2022. Lawyers for Hunter-Carney and Braunskill said they're preparing to defend against the charges. "Was he properly vetted in the city? Who's doing the vetting in Mount Vernon?" community activist Jess Van Lew said Thursday. Mount Vernon to review hiring practices In light of the arrests of Hunter-Carney and Braunskill, Mount Vernon has promised to review its hiring practices and internal affairs follow-up procedures for current and potential employees. Mike Lawlor, a police expert who reviews hiring in New Haven, Connecticut, says it's now vital for Mount Vernon to scrutinize its hiring practices. "I think a rigorous background check is the only way to avoid this type of incident," he said. Lawlor said the goal is to hire candidates who meet the physical, mental and ethical requirements for a demanding job. "They'll look at your social media, other contacts, talk to your neighbors. There's some departments in Connecticut that even require candidates to unlock their phones so they can look at all their text messages, photos, et cetera," he said. Lawlor said many departments today go the extra mile to screen recruits for red flags, including the use of polygraph tests.

articles.title: Want to go solar, but worried by NYC's recent landmark lawsuit? Here's what to know.
articles.author: Mahsa Saeidi; Walter Smith Randolph
articles.article_text: --- title: Want to go solar, but worried by NYC's recent landmark lawsuit? Here's what to know. author: Mahsa Saeidi; Walter Smith Randolph url: https://www.cbsnews.com/newyork/news/solar-power-what-to-know-nyc/ hostname: cbsnews.com description: Here's how New Yorkers can go solar while protecting their pocketbooks. sitename: CBS New York date: 2026-03-13 categories: ['Local News'] tags: ['Solar Power'] --- Last month, New York City announced that CBS News New York's investigative team .Now, investigative reporter Mahsa Saeidi is building on that reporting with a roadmap on how you can go solar, while protecting your pocketbook. Cautionary tale thought solar power would free her from skyrocketing electric bills. Instead, she's still paying several hundred dollars a month, and she says she ended up with a loan she never agreed to. "My best friend has such a program where she didn't pay a cent and she doesn't have a loan for her panels, and her electric bill is about $20," Wynter said. Wynter and her friend live in the same city and use the same technology, but ended up with completely different outcomes. The difference is who they hired. New York City Department of Consumer and Worker Protection (DCWP) Commissioner Samuel Levine said he thinks solar panels can be a great deal, but he advises consumers to slow down. - . Advice for going solar "There are a lot of ways these companies try to trick consumers. Often, for example, they'll quote the monthly cost instead of the full cost," Levine said. The city's consumer watchdog says know the full price of the system: - Be skeptical of free solar claims - Understand what tax credits you actually qualify for: federal, state and city - Shop financing on your own - Don't assume the company's lender is your cheapest option "Often, what's involved there is the solar contractors getting kickbacks," Levine said. If you sign and something feels off, New York law gives you three business days to cancel with no penalty. "If you feel you might have been scammed or you're just not sure, exercise that right," Levine said. Send that cancellation notice in writing. Certified mail is the safest option. A few more tips: make sure your installer is licensed with the DCWP; get at least three written quotes and read every page, and if someone shows up at your door with an iPad and says the offer expires soon, it's not a deadline, it's a warning.

articles.title: School bus driver arrested after driving bus full of children onto train tracks | Video
articles.author: Will Reeve
articles.article_text: --- title: School bus driver arrested after driving bus full of children onto train tracks | Video author: Will Reeve url: https://abc7ny.com/post/school-bus-driver-yvonne-hampton-arrested-driving-full-children-train-tracks-sumter-county-florida-sheriff/18857316/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: A Florida school bus driver was arrested after police say she drove a bus full of children over railroad tracks as a train was approaching. sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2026-04-08 categories: ['crime-safety'] tags: ['18857316', 'school-bus,u.s.-&-world,caught-on-video,caught-on-camera,caught-on-tape,trains,Florida'] --- SUMTER COUNTY, Fla. -- A Florida school bus driver was arrested after police say she drove a bus full of children over railroad tracks as a train was approaching. The incident was caught on camera. The video shows a school bus filled with 29 children being clipped by a fast-moving train at an intersection. The bus driver, Yvonne Hampton, is now out on bond "Trains don't sneak up on people, folks. And that's what led to this arrest, poor judgement that placed children at risk," Sumter County Sheriff Pat Breedon said. Cheyenne Gant, 12, and her mom, Tiffany, say the girl was on the bus with some of her classmates and tried to warn Hampton. SEE ALSO: Car slams into Florida school bus | Video "She like stopped on the tracks, specifically, and then didn't move whenever we were all yelling, 'train!' And it was very scary in the moment," Gant said. According to the arrest report, Hampton claimed, "There was a car at the intersection and she was waiting for the car to move. She said the car began to move. So she began to go over the other side of the tracks. But, the car stopped. The defendant said she then had to stop right where the bus was parked." After reviewing video and audio from the incident, the responding officer says he heard Hampton say "not gonna stop for no train." And she proceeded over the tracks after the railroad crossing warning lights activated, bells sounded and arms lowered, officials said. "I think I'm just going to be like more stressed and like have higher anxiety whenever being on the bus," Gant said. "She's my only daughter; so it's somebody that she could have taken out of (the) world, along with 28 other children that could do great things to the world. And she just had no regard for her actions," Tiffany Gant said.

articles.title: Self Empowered Minds - Upper East Side, NY Patch
articles.article_text: --- title: Self Empowered Minds - Upper East Side, NY Patch url: https://patch.com/new-york/upper-east-side-nyc/business/listing/431083/self-empowered-minds hostname: patch.com description: Saba Hocek and Bill Pratt, members of the Upper East Side for over two decades, are thrilled to announce the fusion of their companies, Self... sitename: Upper East Side, NY Patch date: 2024-04-01 categories: ['Business Listing'] --- About Us Saba Hocek and Bill Pratt, members of the Upper East Side for over two decades, are thrilled to announce the fusion of their companies, Self Empowered Minds and Active Studios NYC. This collaboration marks a significant step towards providing a comprehensive World of Wellness to individuals seeking holistic balance and personal growth. Self Empowered Minds has long been recognized as the premier destination for holistic wellness and personal development. Dedicated to empowering individuals to achieve holistic well-being, the company offers a diverse range of practices including hypnosis, biofeedback, reiki, and intuitive readings. With the integration of Active Studios NYC, renowned for its group fitness classes, Self Empowered Minds has expanded its offerings to include mindfulness classes. From fitness sessions such as Karate, Women’s Self-Defense, Pilates, Rhythmic Somatic workout, Heels and Ballet to mindfulness classes fostering inner peace such as Yoga, Reiki and Breathwork/Sound Bath, a World of Wellness has evolved creating a nurturing environment for growth and development. They welcome any student to try any class the first time at no charge. Beyond physical and mindfulness classes, comprehensive training programs are offered to aspiring practitioners in holistic modalities. The curriculum combines theoretical knowledge with hands-on experience, equipping graduates with the skills necessary to positively impact the lives of others. Whether aspiring to become a Reiki practitioner or a Biological Intuitive Master, Self Empowered Minds provides the platform for transformative learning. Weekly practice space is provided to all certified Reiki practitioners where they can receive, give and share their skills and knowledge with other practitioners. Transform your life with Self Empowered Minds and Active Studios NYC and unlock the potential of holistic wellness. NEW Community Endorsements Community Endorsements NEW

articles.title: Lazare Biomimetic Dentistry And Smile Design - Upper East Side, NY Patch
articles.article_text: --- title: Lazare Biomimetic Dentistry And Smile Design - Upper East Side, NY Patch url: https://patch.com/new-york/upper-east-side-nyc/business/listing/413826/lazare-biomimetic-dentistry-and-smile-design hostname: patch.com description: Lazare Biomimetic Dentistry and Smile Design Studio has been treating patients with the highest level of integrity, comfort, artistry and care... sitename: Upper East Side, NY Patch date: 2024-01-15 categories: ['Business Listing'] --- About Us Lazare Biomimetic Dentistry and Smile Design Studio has been treating patients with the highest level of integrity, comfort, artistry and care since its doors opened in 2011. We brighten, reshape, rebuild, reconstruct, re-align, re-design, maintain and enhance our patient’s teeth in the most biological and minimally invasive way all to help unlock the full potential of their smile. Our Founder and Chief Smile Architect,Dr. Marc Lazare, is a pioneer, leader and educator in the field of Biomimetic dentistry and Smile Design, having authored the book The Patient’s Guide to Biomimetic Dentistry and Smile Design, and the peer-reviewed article titled the Biomimetic Smile Makeover. He has introduced Biomimetic dentistry around the world and has helped to revolutionize modern dentistry with his Biomimetic Smile Makeovers, utilizing the most innovative techniques coupled with the most conservative and minimally invasive procedures. At Lazare Biomimetic Dentistry and Smile Design Studio our goal is to challenge the traditional ways of dentistry with logical and out-side-the-box thinking to avoid, whenever possible, the unnecessary invasive procedures that most traditional dentists perform, such as root canals and crowns. Instead, we use Biomimetic protocols and techniques to save all good tooth structure and rebuild what is missing or broken in a way that mimics the natural properties of the tooth structure being replaced. Our studio offers a variety of treatments and therapies that conventional dental practices do not have. To name a few - Biomimetic general (restorative) dentistry, Biomimetic Smile Makeover, Infrared Light Therapy for TMJ discomfort and Non-invasive laser treatment for cold sores. Visit our website to get to know our team and schedule your appointment in our tranquility oasis. NEW Community Endorsements Community Endorsements NEW

articles.title: Cardi B, Stefon Diggs spark reconciliation rumors amid Offset shooting
articles.author: Jami Ganz
articles.article_text: --- title: Cardi B, Stefon Diggs spark reconciliation rumors amid Offset shooting author: Jami Ganz url: https://www.nydailynews.com/2026/04/09/cardi-b-stefon-diggs-reconciliation-offset-shooting/ hostname: nydailynews.com description: Two days after her estranged husband Offset was shot in Florida, Cardi B and ex Stefon Diggs — who share an infant son — are sparking reconciliation rumors. sitename: New York Daily News date: 2026-04-09 categories: ['SNYDE'] --- Cardi B and Stefon Diggs, who split after welcoming a son this past fall, may be angling for a reconciliation, as he and his family were spotted Wednesday at the Grammy winner’s concert, just days after her estranged husband Offset was wounded in a shooting outside a Florida casino. The wide receiver, 32, was dancing along with his mom and brother during the Washington, D.C. leg of Bronx-raised rapper’s “Little Miss Drama” tour, according to video making the rounds online. Footage from later in the evening shows Diggs, and later, the 33-year-old “Bodak Yellow” artist, being escorted to a car outside the club, Throw Social — where Cardi was hosting the concert’s official after party. The sightings come nearly two months after Cardi, born Belcalis Marlenis, Almánzar, confirmed at one of her shows that she and Diggs had split while also defending him against rapper BIA, who suggested he had “more bms [baby mamas] than receiving yards.” Diggs’ Pats were fresh off their 29-13 Super Bowl loss to the Seattle Seahawks at the time. Still, Cardi said, “Just because I ain’t f–kin’ with my baby daddy doesn’t mean you get to talk about my baby daddy, b—h!” In addition to the son Cardi and Diggs share, he has two kids with two other women. Cardi shares three children, aged 1 to 7, with 34-year-old Offset, real name Kiari Kendrell Cephus. The pair’s tumultuous marriage seemed to end for good with their split in 2024, though their divorce has not yet been finalized. At the time of publication, Cardi appears to have remained mum about the Migos alum’s shooting Monday outside the Seminole Hard Rock Hotel and Casino, and subsequent hospitalization. Insiders Thursday afternoon told TMZ that Cardi reached out to Offset to check in on him on the heels of the shooting. It’s unclear when specifically she made the gesture, and whether they’ve spoken more than once.

articles.title: Family, officials call for safety legislation 1 year after deadly Hudson River helicopter crash
articles.author: Crystal Cranmore
articles.article_text: --- title: Family, officials call for safety legislation 1 year after deadly Hudson River helicopter crash author: Crystal Cranmore url: https://abc7ny.com/post/nyc-helicopter-crash-family-officials-call-legislation-1-year-deadly-hudson-river-chopper-tragedy/18861412/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: The tragedy is sparking calls for increased safety measures for sightseeing flights. sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2026-04-09 categories: ['crime-safety'] tags: ['18861412', 'helicopter-crash,helicopter,hudson-river,New-York-City,Lower-Manhattan'] --- LOWER MANHATTAN (WABC) -- Friday marks one year since a sightseeing helicopter crashed into the Hudson River, killing the pilot and a family of five from Spain. The tragedy has sparked calls for increased safety measures for sightseeing flights. It comes after the New York Helicopter Tours chartered chopper broke apart in the air and crashed into the Hudson River near Lower Manhattan on April 10, 2025. The crash killed pilot Sean Johnson as well as Agustín Escobar Cañadas, Mercè Camprubí Montal, and their three children. The family of the victims from Spain made an emotional visit to the Hudson River on Thursday where they honored their loved ones. But they wanted the visit to also be about turning their grief into action. They stood side by side with local elected officials to push for Congress to pass the Helicopter Safety Parity Act. "We want to avoid more people suffering what we suffered," said family member Joan Camprubí Montal. "And aviation safety for us should not be a choice, it should be a responsibility. Our prayer is that no family will suffer this again." Lawmakers say the legislation aims to close longstanding regulatory loopholes and require sightseeing helicopter operators to meet the same rigorous safety standards as commercial airlines. "It would subject the helicopters to much more rigorous inspection, the same standards as commercial aircraft basically," said Rep. Jerry Nadler. "That would mean inspections more often and more thoroughly." Elected officials say they are still waiting on the official findings from the NTSB on what caused the crash. ---------- Have a breaking news tip or an idea for a story we should cover? Send it to Eyewitness News using the form below. If attaching a video or photo, terms of use apply.

articles.title: Expert warns "AI is being weaponized" as spam calls get more deceptive during tax season
articles.author: Tim McNicholas; Walter Smith Randolph
articles.article_text: --- title: Expert warns "AI is being weaponized" as spam calls get more deceptive during tax season author: Tim McNicholas; Walter Smith Randolph url: https://www.cbsnews.com/newyork/news/ai-spam-calls-tax-season/ hostname: cbsnews.com description: The technology behind spam calls is getting more sophisticated, an expert with the TrueCaller app says. sitename: CBS New York date: 2026-03-19 categories: ['Local News'] tags: ['Artificial Intelligence'] --- If you're getting barraged with spam calls during tax season, you're not alone. It's one of the busiest times for these schemes as people prey on stressed out taxpayers. But if doing your taxes correctly isn't stressful enough this year, experts say is making spam calls trickier to track.They can "synthesize an American accent" Clayton LiaBraaten with the spam call blocking app TrueCaller says the technology is getting more sophisticated. "AI is being weaponized against the consumer. So now I can have better scripts, I can , even if I'm calling from Eastern Europe or East Asia. So it's a very busy time of year for us to try to protect consumers and our users," LiaBraaten said.In 2023, Suffolk County Police told CBS News New York Investigates that an alleged scammer used AI to mimic a real girl's voice into paying thousands of dollars.The technology has only improved since then, as people get spam calls about everything from their taxes to social security benefits. "Scams really can effect anyone. We just noticed in the data that the elder population was losing the most amount of money. In some instances, they were losing their entire life savings," said Rebecca Rose, with the Social Security Administration Office of the Inspector General. "We fight AI with AI" Apps such as Robokiller, Hiya and TrueCaller, work to identify spam callers and then block them or warn users. "We fight AI with AI. We're detecting the synthesized voices, we're detecting the scams, we're detecting the high-volume calls and making sure that our users are protected," LiaBraaten said. Federal government agencies, especially the IRS, tend to communicate via the mail and will rarely, if ever, call you. If you ever have doubt about whether you're actually speaking with a government agency, you can always look up the agency's phone number and reach out directly.

articles.title: Rex Heuermann pleads guilty to murdering 7 women, admits to killing another
articles.author: Adeja Shivonne
articles.article_text: --- title: Rex Heuermann pleads guilty to murdering 7 women, admits to killing another author: Adeja Shivonne url: https://www.fox5ny.com/news/rex-heuermann-gilgo-beach-killings-long-island hostname: fox5ny.com description: Rex Heuermann pleaded guilty on Wednesday in the Gilgo Beach serial killings case, admitting to multiple counts of murder in exchange for consecutive life sentences without the possibility of parole, according to FOX 5 NY's Jodi Goldberg. sitename: FOX 5 New York date: 2026-04-08 tags: ['Us,Ny,Long Island,News,Crime Publicsafety'] --- LONG ISLAND - Rex Heuermann pleaded guilty on Wednesday in the Gilgo Beach serial killings case, admitting to multiple counts of murder in exchange for consecutive life sentences without the possibility of parole, according to FOX 5 NY's Jodi Goldberg. What we know: Heuermann also agreed to waive his right to appeal as part of the plea. He appeared in court in handcuffs and was uncuffed to sign documents formalizing the agreement. Heuermann also agreed to admit he intentionally caused the death of Karen Vergata, though he was not formally charged in her murder. Gilgo Beach murders The backstory: Heuermann was initially arrested in July 2023 and had pleaded not guilty in the deaths of four women known as the "Gilgo Four": Melissa Barthelemy, Maureen Brainard-Barnes, Megan Waterman and Amber Lynn Costello. Their remains were found near each other along Ocean Parkway and were among 10 sets of human remains discovered in the area between 2010 and 2011. Prosecutors later charged Heuermann in additional killings, including Sandra Costilla and Jessica Taylor, both of whom were long believed to be victims of a serial predator targeting sex workers. Authorities have also tied him to the killing of Valerie Mack. The victims linked to the case span decades: - Sandra Costilla, who disappeared in 1993 - Valerie Mack, who disappeared in 2000 - Jessica Taylor, who disappeared in 2003 - Maureen Brainard-Barnes, who disappeared in 2007 - Melissa Barthelemy, who disappeared in 2009 - Megan Waterman, who disappeared in 2010 - Amber Lynn Costello, who disappeared in 2010 The case, which remained unsolved for years, gained renewed momentum after investigators used DNA evidence and cellphone data to link Heuermann to the killings.

articles.title: This Sanders-backed progressive is aiming for a seat in Congress: Who is Analilia Mejia?
articles.author: Briana Scalia
articles.article_text: --- title: This Sanders-backed progressive is aiming for a seat in Congress: Who is Analilia Mejia? author: Briana Scalia url: https://www.fox5ny.com/election/new-jersey-special-election-analilia-mejia-mikie-sherrill-congress-progressive-trump hostname: fox5ny.com description: Democratic candidate Analilia Mejia is gunning for Gov. Mikie Sherrill's old seat in Congress. Here's a closer look at her past, and what she's planning for New Jersey's future. sitename: FOX 5 New York date: 2026-04-08 tags: ['Politics,Election,Us,Nj'] --- NEW JERSEY - Democratic candidate Analilia Mejia is gunning for Gov. Mikie Sherrill's old seat in Congress. Here's a closer look at her past, and what she's planning for New Jersey's future. Analilia Mejia, US Democratic House candidate for New Jersey, speaks to supporters and members of the media at Paper Plane Coffee Co. in Montclair, New Jersey, US, on Thursday, Jan. 29, 2026. Mejia is one of 11 Democrats vying to fill the House seat Who is Analilia Mejia? What we know: Mejia was born in New Jersey to Colombian and Dominican immigrants. After starting as a union organizer, Mejia served as the National Political Director for Sen. Bernie Sanders’ 2020 presidential campaign, and in the Department of Labor under former President Joe Biden. She is currently the Co-Executive Director of Popular Democracy, a network of grassroots organizations across the country. Issues, platform Local perspective: Two of the most prevalent issues listed on Mejia's campaign website include canceling student loan debt and calling for the abolition of Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). The Democratic nominee also mentions holding President Donald Trump and his allies "accountable for their corruption." Other issues Mejia highlights in her campaign platform include: - Guaranteeing paid sick leave on a federal level - Providing Medicare for All - Supporting articles of impeachment against Supreme Court Justices Clarence Thomas and Samuel Alito Endorsements As of now, Mejia touts endorsements from Sen. Bernie Sanders, Sen. Elizabeth Warren, Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, Newark Mayor Ras Baraka and several other politicians. The Source: This article includes information from Analilia Mejia's campaign website.

articles.article_text: --- title: AccuWeather: Cold start, warmer PM author: Eyewitness News url: https://abc7ny.com/post/nyc-weather-forecast-7-day-temperatures/27410/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: Another cold morning on Thursday, but the warmup begins to kick in very gradually starting in the afternoon, with a light wind and temperatures around the mid-50s. sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2020-02-12 categories: ['weather'] tags: ['nyc weather, accuweather forecast, new york city weather, weather forecast, tri-state weather, heat wave, hot, air quality, air quality alert, sam champion, lee goldberg, jeff smith, brittany bell, dani beckstrom, 27410', 'weather,accuweather,forecast,nyc-weather,rain,snow,cold,heat,spring,summer,fall,winter,storm,drought,New-York-City'] --- AccuWeather forecast for NYC, New York, New Jersey and Connecticut NEW YORK (WABC) -- Another cold morning on Thursday, but the warmup begins to kick in very gradually starting in the afternoon, with a light wind and temperatures around the mid-50s. Friday will be milder as sun mixes with clouds. There will be a late-night shower. Morning clouds give way to sun on Saturday, with temperatures in the mid-60s. Sunday will be in the lower 60s as sun mixes with clouds ahead of a warm front that comes through at night with a passing shower. There will be a lot of clouds mixing with sunshine on Monday, but we still will get in the mid- to-upper-70s. Then it's a summer preview on Tuesday with temperatures around 80. Wednesday is still warm but with an afternoon thunder threat. Thursday Cold start, warmer PM. High 54. Friday Milder, late night shower. High 67. Saturday Clearing and pleasant. High 66. Sunday Sun and PM clouds. High 60. Monday Breezy and warm. High 77. Tuesday Summer preview. High 82. Wednesday PM t-storm. High 80. Please fill out the form below to submit weather photos and videos. articles.title: Michael Patrick, 'Game of Thrones' actor, dies at 35
articles.author: Heather Miller
articles.article_text: --- title: Michael Patrick, 'Game of Thrones' actor, dies at 35 author: Heather Miller url: https://www.fox5ny.com/news/michael-patrick-game-thrones-actor-dies-35 hostname: fox5ny.com description: Actor and writer Michael Patrick has died. sitename: FOX 5 New York date: 2026-04-09 tags: ['Entertainment,News'] --- Michael Patrick, the Irish actor and writer who played Richard III in a wheelchair and appeared on an episode of "Game of Thrones," has died. He was 35 years old. Michael Patrick’s death What we know: Patrick, whose real name was Michael Cambell, died in hospice care, his wife, Naomi Sheehan, confirmed in a social media post. She said he was admitted to hospice after a battle with motor neuron disease. What we don't know: It’s unclear what type of Motor Neuron Disease he had, but the most common form is ALS. RELATED: Celebrity deaths of 2026: The entertainers and artists we lost this year What they're saying: "Words can’t describe how broken-hearted we are," Sheehan said on Instagram. "It’s been said more than once that Mick was an inspiration to everyone who was privileged enough to come into contact with him, not just in the past few years during his illness but in every day of his life. He lived a life as full as any human can live. Joy, abundance of spirit, infectious laughter. A titan of a ginger haired man. "We are so grateful for every person who supported us through the last few years." She added one of her husband’s favorite quotes from Brendan Behan: "The most important things to do in the world are to get something to eat, something to drink and somebody to love you. "So, don’t overthink it," she continued. "Eat. Drink. Love." Michael Patrick’s work Patrick was best known in the U.S. for appearing in a season 6 episode of "Game of Thrones," but according to BBC, he was widely acclaimed in his home country for playing Richard III in a wheelchair in Shakespeare's "Richard III." What is Motor Neuron Disease? The backstory: According to the MND Association, motor neuron disease "affects the nerves known as motor neurons," which "help tell your muscles what to do." The Source: This article includes information from Naomi Sheehan’s Instagram account and the BBC.

articles.title: NASA's next Artemis missions being planned with help of N.Y. professor: "I was over the moon!"
articles.author: Jennifer McLogan
articles.article_text: --- title: NASA's next Artemis missions being planned with help of N.Y. professor: "I was over the moon!" author: Jennifer McLogan url: https://www.cbsnews.com/newyork/news/artemis-nasa-future-moon-missions-timothy-glotch/ hostname: cbsnews.com description: Stony Brook University professor Timothy Glotch is one of 10 scientists selected to shape research plans for NASA's next Artemis missions. sitename: CBS New York date: 2026-04-03 categories: ['Local News'] tags: ['Suffolk County, NASA, Featured Stories & Web Exclusives'] --- Shortly after the on its trip around the moon, a college professor in New York learned he was picked by NASA to help with future missions.Stony Brook University professor Timothy Glotch is one of just 10 scientists from across the country selected to shape research plans for the next .The call of a lifetime Glotch got the call of his life , when NASA exploration leaders told the Long Island planetary geologist that he and nine others would be bringing their wealth of experience to the ."I was over the moon!" he said. "My heart was racing, I couldn't wipe the smile off my face for the entire day." Glotch will help astronauts on the 2028 Artemis mission locate minerals and water ice near the moon's south pole, and start to build a base on the moon in the coming years. "Artemis is being built from the ground up to be sustainable," he said. "I'm excited to bring Artemis to Stony Brook and teach classes." Planning next moon launch Glotch will be front and center for the 2028 launch to the moon from either Cape Canaveral, Florida, or the Johnson Space Center in Houston, Texas. While discussing his elation, Glotch donned gloves - a strict protocol - and showed lunar dust experiments from rock samples collected on the surface of the 4.5 billion-year-old moon by Apollo astronauts more than 50 years ago. "We can take this tiny little amount of sample and use it to inform our global perspective," he said. "By studying the moon, we learn more about the Earth." All of his research is being done on Earth, but would Glotch ever want to take a rocket on a trip to the moon? "I don't even like tall roller coasters," he said. "I'm happy to do my science here on Earth."

articles.title: Mamdani administration releases NYC's 1st Preliminary Citywide Racial Equity Plan and True Cost of Living reports
articles.author: Jesse Zanger
articles.article_text: --- title: Mamdani administration releases NYC's 1st Preliminary Citywide Racial Equity Plan and True Cost of Living reports author: Jesse Zanger url: https://www.cbsnews.com/newyork/news/nyc-preliminary-citywide-racial-equity-plan-true-cost-of-living-reports/ hostname: cbsnews.com description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani's administration released New York City's first Preliminary Citywide Racial Equity Plan and True Cost of Living Measure. sitename: CBS New York date: 2026-04-06 categories: ['Local News'] tags: ['Zohran Mamdani'] --- Mayor Zohran Mamdani's administration released New York City's first Preliminary Citywide Racial Equity Plan and True Cost of Living Measure. The reports were mandated by .The Mamdani administration says the two reports "make clear that New York City's affordability crisis is deeply tied to its history of racial inequity," and cited "patterns of disinvestment, exclusion from homeownership, unequal access to health care" and more as factors. "This Preliminary Racial Equity Plan is the first step in developing a whole-of-government approach to tackling that reality. It is a plan that lays out these first steps to solve decades of neglect and discrimination, and it places the work of 45 city agencies within a singular framework," Mamdani said. "Inequity has been embedded in the foundation of our city and nation since their inception; dismantling it requires a collective effort," said NYC Chief Equity Officer and NYC Mayor's Office of Equity & Racial Justice Commissioner Afua Atta-Mensah. Goals of the the Preliminary Citywide Racial Equity Plan include advancing pay equity, anti-racism training for city government staff and improving data collection across various demographics. The TCOL report found 62% of New Yorkers don't make enough to cover their basic living expenses. The report found the median annual costs for a family with children are $159,197, but median resources are $124,007, meaning there's a greater than $35,000 gap. The reports come at a time when the in federal government.U.S. Assistant Attorney General for Civil Rights Harmeet Dhillon responded to the reports on social media. "Sounds fishy/illegal. Will review!" she wrote.

articles.title: Dog snatched from family's backyard safely returned
articles.author: Anthony Johnson
articles.article_text: --- title: Dog snatched from family's backyard safely returned author: Anthony Johnson url: https://abc7ny.com/post/rocky-dog-snatched-familys-backyard-safely-returned/18857657/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: Rocky, the dog that was snatched from his backyard in Clifton, New Jersey, by a woman and child, has been found safe. sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2026-04-08 categories: ['pets-animals'] tags: ['18857657', 'dogs-stolen,surveillance-video,lost-pet,dog,Clifton'] --- CLIFTON, NEW JERSEY (WABC) -- Rocky, the dog that was snatched from his backyard in Clifton, New Jersey, by a woman and child, has been found safe. After he was dognapped on Easter Sunday, Rocky was found and reunited with his family. "Such a great feeling that we recovered him and that he's going to go back home," a family member, Ronnie Villalovos, said. Ring video captured the shocking moment when a woman and child snatched Rocky from the back porch of his home. His family was devastated by his disappearance. On Wednesday, they are relieved by his return. The Chan family has had Rocky for seven years and the last few days have been filled with confusion and sadness. Now, they are celebrating Rocky's return. "I'm really happy. I've been really stressed out these past few days," Chen's daughter said. Police are reportedly talking to the woman who took the dog after following up on the Honda Pilot that was seen during the incident. The Chan family says she is a resident of Newark and was apparently making deliveries in Clifton when she spotted Rocky. The Yorki mix had dashed out the door when another family member walked inside the home. The woman was seen on camera grabbing the dog from his own yard after her had just been outside for a few minutes. His family says they plan on spoiling him after the harrowing experience. "Definitely steak, he loves steak. Whatever he wants. We'll probably get him a steak bigger than him," the family said. At this point, there are no criminal charges. articles.title: Boxcar Burgers to open in Smith Haven Mall
articles.author: Celia Young
articles.article_text: --- title: Boxcar Burgers to open in Smith Haven Mall author: Celia Young url: https://www.newsday.com/business/boxcar-burgers-smith-haven-mall-wgpy56ki hostname: newsday.com description: It will be the Ronkonkoma-based fast casual eateries second location. sitename: Newsday date: 2026-04-07 categories: ['Business'] tags: ['Business, Real Estate, Restaurants'] --- A new burger joint is headed to the food court of Smith Haven Mall in Lake Grove. The fast-casual eatery Boxcar Burgers will open in the mall this summer, after launching outside the Ronkonkoma terminus of the Long Island Rail Road in 2020, co-owners Steve Cannon and Michael Dorian said. The two longtime friends and business partners have not yet secured permits to begin construction on the roughly 1,000-square-foot space, which formerly belonged to a Duchess Cookies, Cannon said. They plan to build and staff the store by July, with about 15 to 20 employees, Dorian said. The new restaurant comes six years after the duo launched their burger business in January 2020, just six weeks before the COVID-19 pandemic shutdown, Newsday reported. Cannon and Dorian, both longtime veterans of the restaurant business, said that offering pickup and delivery helped keep the Ronkonkoma location afloat. "2020, with COVID, that year was a wash," Cannon said. "But in the past year or two, it’s not that anybody forgot about COVID, but you could comfortably feel that it’s in the rear view mirror." They had wanted to open at the mall a few years ago, but at the time, the space was "a little too big for our britches," Dorian said. After considering a handful of other locations, Cannon said they reached back out to the mall last year to see if space was available — and it was. "We always liked the idea of being" at the mall, because "of the volume and the foot traffic and the exposure," Cannon said. Dorian said they hope to expand further across Long Island in the future, though right now, they’re focused on the Smith Haven outpost. "We want to keep expanding," Dorian said. A representative for Smith Haven Mall did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

articles.title: Prainam Thai closes in Port Washington
articles.author: Andi Berlin
articles.article_text: --- title: Prainam Thai closes in Port Washington author: Andi Berlin url: https://www.newsday.com/lifestyle/restaurants/prainam-thai-closes-in-port-washington-rszidtie hostname: newsday.com description: Their Top 50 location is still in business. sitename: Newsday date: 2026-04-07 categories: ['Things to Do'] tags: ['Restaurants, FeedMe, FMClose'] --- Prainam Thai was a promising new restaurant in Port Washington from the owners of a Newsday Top 50 restaurant. But the ambitious concept closed at the end of March after just over one year following a money dispute between co-owners, the business partners said. Married couple Vorragun "Brian" Vongdarunee and Kasamaporn "Masi" Chansaksri are the duo behind Tonnam Thai in Bohemia, a hidden gem spot not much larger than a takeout place that serves highly executed renditions of Bangkok street foods. Shortly after Tonnam got on Newsday's radar, the couple partnered with real estate broker Oakar Reinstein to open Prainam, a flashy date night spot on Main Street in Port Washington in late 2024. Reinstein is originally from Myanmar and also owns a KC's Pizza and Wings in Islip Terrace and Holtsville. For Prainam, he brought in Thai servers from Queens and Manhattan to help customers navigate a large menu with crowd-pleasers and harder-to-find Thai dishes. The crispy duck curry, with an ample portion of tender duck breast in a red curry, was a highlight on the menu. But a financial dispute led both parties to part ways and close, the co-owners said. Chansaksri confirmed that she'd sold off the concept to new owners, who plan to open a Thai-Mexican fusion spot. She and her husband are still running the original Tonnam, and also plan to look for a new location with more ample seating that they can run without another partner. Reinstein on the other hand, said he wants to take some time off from the restaurant business, and focus more on his housing properties on Long Island and upstate. "I might do another Thai place, but it’s not something that I’m thinking about right now," Reinstein said.

articles.title: Fireworks may have caused portable toilet explosion in Yonkers park: police
articles.author: Marcus Solis
articles.article_text: --- title: Fireworks may have caused portable toilet explosion in Yonkers park: police author: Marcus Solis url: https://abc7ny.com/post/portable-toilet-explodes-yonkers-bregano-park-police-believe-fireworks-may-have-caused-blast/18853209/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: The destruction, which occurred around 8:20 p.m. in Bregano Park, prompted a significant response, complete with agents in protective suits collecting evidence. sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2026-04-07 categories: ['crime-safety'] tags: ['18853209', 'explosion,toilet,police,FBI,Westchester-County,Yonkers'] --- YONKERS, New York (WABC) -- Police are investigating after a portable toilet exploded in a park in Yonkers Monday night. The destruction, which occurred around 8:20 p.m. in Bregano Park, prompted a significant response, complete with agents in protective suits collecting evidence. The entire perimeter of the park has been cordoned off. The focus of attention is a blue porta potty that has now been blasted to pieces. They believe whoever did it used fireworks. "Last night, it sounded like a bomb," said resident Maria Sigismondo. "I was sitting in the living room and I went, 'oh my god.' The whole floor shook. And then my neighbors, you know, and everybody were just talking about it. Residents in the area say there have been four or five similar explosions over the last few weeks, usually around the same time after nightfall. "At first, we thought it was like fireworks. Then it got louder and bigger, but last night was really big," said resident Kenny Reyes. "And now this." Yonkers Police contacted the county bomb squad which responded along with the FBI and ATF. "I am pretty concerned, especially when you see the FBI, but hopefully they get to the bottom of it. You know, you worry about it," Sigismondo said. "The world today is a little strange." ---------- Have a breaking news tip or an idea for a story we should cover? Send it to Eyewitness News using the form below. If attaching a video or photo, terms of use apply.

articles.title: Jonah Hill secretly married partner, welcomes baby number 2
articles.author: Jessica Schladebeck
articles.article_text: --- title: Jonah Hill secretly married partner, welcomes baby number 2 author: Jessica Schladebeck url: https://www.nydailynews.com/2026/04/09/jonah-hill-secretly-married-partner-welcomes-baby-number-2/ hostname: nydailynews.com description: Jonah Hill has confirmed he secretly married partner Olivia Millar, who also recently just gave birth to their second child. sitename: New York Daily News date: 2026-04-09 categories: ['SNYDE'] --- Jonah Hill has confirmed he secretly married partner Olivia Millar, who also recently just gave birth to their second child. The “Superbad” star made the double reveal during a sit down with Martin Scorsese for Interview magazine, published on Thursday. While speaking about his latest directorial effort, the upcoming movie “Outcome,” Hill explained that it takes a special type of project to pull him away from his family. “I have two kids now,” he said. “The only thing that could ever separate me from my family is the editing room. I love the writing, I love the shooting, but editing— it’s like dessert every day. Even the problems are dessert.” Elsewhere in the article, he referred to Millar as his “wife.” “My biggest fear in doing this [interview] was that you’d have to lie about liking the movie,” Hill told Scorsese. “I was saying to my wife, ‘He’s seeing it over the weekend, so what if he hates it but already agreed to do it?'” His rep also confirmed the news to People, adding that the 42-year-old actor is “happy.” Hill and Millar were first linked in September 2022, after they were seen spending time together in Santa Barbara. Almost a year later, they welcomed their first child together. The “Moneyball” actor was previously engaged to Gianna Santos, but they ultimately parted ways in October 2020. He was then linked to surfer Sarah Brady, who alleged on Instagram in 2023 that Hill was emotionally abusive during their relationship. They were rumored to be engaged in 2022, but Hill quickly shut down the claims.

articles.title: Olympian Alyssa Liu wants to become a professional piercer
articles.author: Brian Niemietz
articles.article_text: --- title: Olympian Alyssa Liu wants to become a professional piercer author: Brian Niemietz url: https://www.nydailynews.com/2026/03/17/olympian-alyssa-liu-wants-to-become-a-professional-piercer/ hostname: nydailynews.com description: Gold medal winning figure skater Alyssa Liu hopes to get a license in piercing. The 20-year-old Olympian has already pierced her own ear and mouth. sitename: New York Daily News date: 2026-03-17 categories: ['National News'] tags: ['Alyssa Liu, piercing, Olympics, figure skater, gold medal, Vanity Fair'] --- Olympic gold medalist Alyssa Liu plans to get licensed to pierce people. The 20-year-old figure skater confessed she’s already pierced herself multiple times and at least one other person as well. “I did my own helix and my third lobes too,” Liu said in a video shot while she prepared to attend Sunday’s Vanity Fair Oscar party. “I actually want to get licensed.” Liu proudly showed holes she put at the top and bottom of her ear lobes as well as other ear piercings. Asked if she’s pierced anyone else, she coyly held her index finger over her lips, nodded and grinned. Liu told Vanity Fair that her unique style is inspired by her siblings, most notably her brother Justin, with whom she shares clothing. Three of those siblings are triplets, according to People. She showed off the earrings she planned to wear the party, then addressed her “smiley” — a dental piercing she previously discussed while participating in last month’s Milano Cortina Olympic games. “I had my sister hold-up my lip and I was, like, looking in the mirror and I had my piercing needle… and yeah, I just put it through,” Liu told NBC affiliate TMJ4 . She said that particular piercing, which everyone saw when Liu smiled during the Olympic games, is about 2 years old. Liu became one of the brightest stars of that competition by winning gold in a personal and team event just four years after announcing her retirement from figure skating.

articles.title: Brooklyn girl, 3, clings to life after darting away from mom, struck by SUV driver
articles.author: Thomas Tracy
articles.article_text: --- title: Brooklyn girl, 3, clings to life after darting away from mom, struck by SUV driver author: Thomas Tracy url: https://www.nydailynews.com/2026/04/09/brooklyn-girl-3-clings-to-life-after-darting-away-from-mom-struck-by-suv-driver/ hostname: nydailynews.com description: The girl stepped away from the mom on the sidewalk and darted between two parked cars onto 50th St. near Fifth Ave. in Sunset Park. sitename: New York Daily News date: 2026-04-09 categories: ['Crime and Public Safety'] --- A 3-year-old Brooklyn girl is clinging to life after she broke away from her mother, ran into traffic and was struck by a passing SUV driver early Thursday, police said. The girl stepped away from the mom on the sidewalk and darted between two parked cars onto 50th St. near Fifth Ave. in Sunset Park just after 12 a.m., horrified witnesses told cops. The child was hit by a passing 2018 Nissan Sentra operated by a 43-year-old man and carrying two girls, one 11, one 9, cops said. The near-fatal strike is hauntingly similar to a scene that played out a month earlier when a 4-year-old boy broke away from his mother and was fatally struck by a passing SUV outside a Brooklyn hospital. In Thursday’s crash, the tot’s family rushed her to Bellevue Hospital, where she is in critical condition. No one in the Nissan was hurt. The driver remained on the scene and faced no immediate charges. The NYPD’s Collision Investigation Squad is investigating the crash. On March 5, 4-year-old Zachariah Padilla was fatally hit by a Ford SUV driver outside of Brookdale University Hospital on Rockaway Parkway and Linden Blvd. The child had just broken away from his mother, Harmonie Wright, who was chasing after him when he was hit about 11:10 a.m., cops said. The child’s panicked mother grabbed him and rushed him into Brookdale University Hospital but he couldn’t be saved. The SUV driver sped off. No arrests have been made.

articles.title: Queens man charged with murder, arson for fire that killed 4
articles.author: Joe Calabrese
articles.article_text: --- title: Queens man charged with murder, arson for fire that killed 4 author: Joe Calabrese url: https://www.fox5ny.com/news/queens-fire-4-killed-suspect-arrested-april-8-2026 hostname: fox5ny.com description: A Queens man has been charged with setting a fire at an apartment building that killed four people, including a 3-year-old girl. sitename: FOX 5 New York date: 2026-04-09 tags: ['Crime Publicsafety,News,Us,Ny,Nyc,Queens'] --- QUEENS - The NYPD has arrested a suspect accused of setting a fire at a Queens apartment building that killed four people last month. Queens fire suspect arrested What we know: Police arrested 38-year-old Roman Amatitla on Wednesday, April 8. Amatitla has been charged with murder, arson, assault and petit larceny in connection with the fire. Roman Amatitla The backstory: The fire happened on March 16 at an apartment building on Avery Avenue in Flushing. Firefighters responded to the building to find heavy flames. Crews reported seeing three people jump from an upper floor as the flames spread. PREVIOUS COVERAGE: Queens fire that killed 4 ruled a homicide: Police After the fire was put out, firefighters found three people, a 3-year-old girl, a 50-year-old man and a 61-year-old woman, dead inside. Paramedics also brought a 63-year-old man to the hospital, where he later died from his injuries. The Office of the Chief Medical Examiner later ruled the fire a homicide. Two victims identified Dig deeper: So far, two of the four victims have been identified. Three-year-old Sihan Yang and 50-year-old Chengri Cui were among those found dead in the building. What we don't know: Officials have not released the identities of the other two victims. The Fire Marshal is still working to determine the exact cause of the fire. What's next: Amatitla is being held without bail. The Source: Information in this story is from the NYPD and previous FOX 5 NY reports.

articles.title: UES Restaurateur To Open New Seafood-Focused Wine Bar
articles.author: Miranda Levingston
articles.article_text: --- title: UES Restaurateur To Open New Seafood-Focused Wine Bar author: Miranda Levingston url: https://patch.com/new-york/upper-east-side-nyc/ues-restaurateur-open-new-seafood-focused-wine-bar hostname: patch.com description: Something fresh is coming to Third Avenue. sitename: Patch date: 2026-04-09 categories: ['Restaurants & Bars'] --- UES Restaurateur To Open New Seafood-Focused Wine Bar Something fresh is coming to Third Avenue. UPPER EAST SIDE, NY — Local restaurateur Enrique Lerma is planning a new seafood and wine bar concept on Third Avenue, he told Upper East Side's Community Board 8 this week. Lerma, who also co-owns Bar Vivant on East 88th Street and Alison on East 104th Street, came before Community Board 8's Street Life Committee on Tuesday night to share his plans and apply for a liquor license. He told the committee that the new restaurant, which is yet to be named, will be open seven days a week, from 11 a.m. to 2 a.m. at 1614 Third Ave. between East 90th and East 91st streets. Find out what's happening in Upper East Sidefor free with the latest updates from Patch. "This is where the old Kaia Wine Bar used to be," Lerma said, mentioning the neighborhood's only South African restaurant, which moved to First Avenue between East 75th and East 76th streets in late January after a 15-year run on Third Avenue. "Very similar concept, seafood-based," Lerma said. Find out what's happening in Upper East Sidefor free with the latest updates from Patch. Like Kaia, the restaurant will serve mostly wine and cocktails seven days a week, Lerma said. The committee unanimously approved the liquor license. Now, the final decision rests with the New York State Liquor Authority. No opening timeline has been released yet. For questions, email Miranda.Levingston@Patch.com. Get more local news delivered straight to your inbox. Sign up for free Patch newsletters and alerts.

articles.title: Neglected dog 'left to die' on side of road in New Jersey, rescuers say
articles.article_text: --- title: Neglected dog 'left to die' on side of road in New Jersey, rescuers say url: https://abc7ny.com/post/monmouth-county-spca-cares-abandoned-dog-left-side-road-marlboro-nj/18834846/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: The shelter said the pup "was discarded like trash, unable to walk and skeletal from starvation." sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2026-04-03 categories: ['pets-animals'] tags: ['18834846', 'dog,pets,shelter,animal-rescue,Monmouth-County,Marlboro'] --- MONMOUTH COUNTY, New Jersey (WABC) -- The Monmouth County SPCA is helping to care for a neglected dog that they say was left to die on the side of a road in Marlboro. Shelter officials said they found Firefox left in a crate and unable to walk earlier this week. He weighed only 3 pounds when he was rescued. In a Facebook post, the shelter said the pup "was discarded like trash, unable to walk and skeletal from starvation." He had a body condition score of one, which is the lowest possible rating before death. Firefox's blood sugar levels are four times the normal limit due to trauma. Rescuers said he survived his first critical night in emergency care, but his road to recovery is long, as he is experiencing medical issues on top of starvation and neglect. Monmouth County SPCA is seeking donations, as Firefox has a long road to recovery. They said they need extra support for his hospitalization, physical therapy, specialized food regimen for weight gain and ongoing medical care. While law enforcement searches for the person responsible for abandoning Firefox, the shelter is asking if anyone knows where Firefox came from to reach out to the Marlboro Police Department or email them at info@monmouthcountyspca.org. ---------- Have a breaking news tip or an idea for a story we should cover? Send it to Eyewitness News using the form below. If attaching a video or photo, terms of use apply.

articles.title: Iran live updates: Iran says Hormuz is open to all ships that coordinate with Tehran
articles.article_text: --- title: Iran live updates: Iran says Hormuz is open to all ships that coordinate with Tehran url: https://abc7ny.com/live-updates/iran-war-strait-hormuz-ceasefire-trump-stock-market/18847792/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: President Donald Trump says he has agreed to suspend a planned bombing attack on Iran for two weeks if Iran agrees to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. sitename: ABC OTV Website date: 2026-04-09 tags: ['18847792'] --- President Donald Trump said Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu will "ease up" Israel's strikes in Lebanon and be more "low key," in a phone interview with an Israeli journalist on Thursday. This comes as Iran warned this morning that negotiations with the U.S. could be "meaningless" if Israel's attacks on Lebanon continue. "Netanyahu is going to take a step back on the Lebanon issue," Trump reportedly said. "Netanyahu will be fine with Lebanon. He'll ease up a bit there. He has a problem with Hezbollah but he'll ease up and be totally fine." This phone call took place before Netanyahu authorized direct talks with Lebanon to begin, according to the reporter. In the Thursday phone interview, the president also expressed optimism over reaching a "very successful agreement," seemingly looking towards the U.S. and Iran talks that are scheduled to begin Saturday in Islamabad, Pakistan. "We're going to have a very successful agreement. It's gonna be very good, everything's gonna work out very good," Trump said. Trump claimed that Netanyahu is "on board" with the ceasefire agreement. Netanyahu "supports the ceasefire agreement with Iran. He's totally fine with it," Trump said, according to the reporter. ABC News has asked the White House about reports that say Trump had specifically asked Netanyahu to scale back Israeli strikes on Lebanon in a phone call between the two leaders Wednesday evening.

articles.title: Original cast brings dose of nostalgia with revival of 'Malcolm in the Middle: Life's Still Unfair'
articles.article_text: --- title: Original cast brings dose of nostalgia with revival of 'Malcolm in the Middle: Life's Still Unfair' url: https://abc7ny.com/post/malcolm-middle-lifes-unfair-red-carpet-premiere-held-nyc/18854369/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: Almost all of the original cast of "Malcolm in the Middle" is back, including Frankie Muniz, who plays Malcolm. sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2026-04-08 categories: ['entertainment'] tags: ['18854369', 'synergy,Synergy---Hulu,hulu,New-York-City'] --- NEW YORK -- "Malcolm in the Middle" is back! Joelle Garguilo spoke to the cast at the Red Carpet in New York City on Tuesday, including Frankie Muniz, Bryan Cranston and Jane Kaczmarek who are returning for "Malcolm in the Middle: Life's Still Unfair." The popular TV show is coming back for a four-episode revival. The original show premiered more than 20 years ago. Almost all of the original cast is back, including Frankie Muniz, who plays Malcolm, and says he was thrilled to come back. "I love it. I could play Malcolm forever. You know what I mean? A lot of actors are like 'oh I don't want to do this anymore,' this was awesome," Muniz said. "Everyone loves the show." "There is intrinsic value in pure laughter," said Bryan Cranston, who plays Malcom's dad in the show. "Joy and laughter can actually make your day better. Maybe make your week better. It's not a panacea. It doesn't solve problems, but it does lift you up to take another step and another day." "We've been given a gift, and we're we're celebrating. Who knew? I certainly didn't have any inkling that we'd ever revisit these characters again, and here we are," Cranston said. "Malcolm in the Middle: Life's Still Unfair" premieres April 10 on Hulu. The Walt Disney Company is the parent company of Hulu and this station.

articles.title: Singer Pink to host 79th Annual Tony Awards at NYC's iconic Radio City Music Hall
articles.author: Mark Prussin
articles.article_text: --- title: Singer Pink to host 79th Annual Tony Awards at NYC's iconic Radio City Music Hall author: Mark Prussin url: https://www.cbsnews.com/newyork/news/pink-to-host-2026-tony-awards/ hostname: cbsnews.com description: The 3-time Grammy Award-winner will host the 2026 Tonys live on CBS on June 7 in New York City. sitename: CBS New York date: 2026-04-09 categories: ['Local News'] tags: ['Tony Awards, Pink'] --- Pop star Pink will host the 79th Annual Tony Awards which will crown the best in Broadway for the 2025-26 season, Tony Award Productions announced Thursday. The 3-time Grammy Award-winner will lead the 2026 Tonys live on June 7 at Radio City Music Hall in New York City. "It is the honor of an entire lifetime to host a night celebrating the literal hardest working people in showbiz," Pink said in a statement. "Broadway has shaped my life and how I put my own shows together - it is a community that is supportive, and inclusive, and full of talent and love. These people give magic every single day, and I cannot wait to celebrate them with the entire world." She also posted a video confirming the news on Instagram. 2026 Tonys executive producers Raj Kapoor, Sarah Levine Hall and Jack Sussman described her as "a fearless artist whose powerhouse voice, electrifying stage presence, and undeniable authenticity embody the very spirit of live performance and theater." In addition to her Grammys, she has won a Daytime Emmy, seven MTV Video Music Awards and has had 15 Top 10 hits on the Billboard Hot 100. Two of her songs are featured in current Broadway productions of "Moulin Rouge! The Musical" and "& Juliet." The 2026 Tony Awards air live at 8 p.m. ET on June 7 on CBS and Paramount+. Last year, "Wicked" star hosted the .

articles.title: LIRR workers could go on strike next month if they cannot reach a deal
articles.author: Eyewitness News
articles.article_text: --- title: LIRR workers could go on strike next month if they cannot reach a deal author: Eyewitness News url: https://abc7ny.com/post/mta-faces-potential-transit-disruptions-ahead-may-16-lirr-strike-deadline-looms/18860703/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: Long Island Rail Road workers could go on strike as early as May 16 if they cannot reach a deal. sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2026-04-09 categories: ['traffic'] tags: ['18860703', 'lirr,mta,new-york-city-transit,mass-transit,union-contract,labor-unions,negotiations,New-York-City'] --- NEW YORK (WABC) -- Long Island Rail Road workers could go on strike as early as May 16 if they cannot reach a deal. The leaders of five unions who represent more than 3,500 LIRR workers held a briefing Wednesday. They want a retroactive 9.5% wage increase that covers the past three years. They also want an additional 5% raise starting this year. The federal government prevent a work stoppage, but Congress did not intervene last year when NJ Transit workers went on strike. LIRR workers last went on strike in 1994. At the same time, contract talks with Transport Workers Union Local 100, which represents subway and bus operators, are getting underway. Negotiations officially begin Thursday with an exchange of demands at the union's Brooklyn headquarters. Union leaders say rising costs and workplace conditions will be the center of discussions. A news conference scheduled for Thursday afternoon is expected to provide more details on the union's demands and the path forward in negotiations. ---------- Have a breaking news tip or an idea for a story we should cover? articles.title: Brooklyn woman, 76, killed when SUV crashes into building at Floyd Bennett Field
articles.author: Thomas Tracy
articles.article_text: --- title: Brooklyn woman, 76, killed when SUV crashes into building at Floyd Bennett Field author: Thomas Tracy url: https://www.nydailynews.com/2026/04/09/brooklyn-woman-76-killed-when-suv-crashes-into-building-at-floyd-bennett-field/ hostname: nydailynews.com description: Pearle Davis was riding in the back of a 2022 Toyota RAV4 when the 33-year-old woman behind the wheel lost control about 6:10 p.m. Wednesday. sitename: New York Daily News date: 2026-04-09 categories: ['Crime and Public Safety'] --- A 76-year-old woman was killed when the SUV she was riding in slammed into the side of a building at Brooklyn’s Floyd Bennett Field, police said Thursday. Pearle Davis was riding in the back of a 2022 Toyota RAV4 when the 33-year-old woman behind the wheel lost control about 6:10 p.m. Wednesday, cops said. The driver veered off the roadway inside Floyd Bennet Field and slammed into the side of a hangar now used as a warehouse off Flatbush Ave. in Marine Park. Floyd Bennett Field was the city’s first municipal airport. It became part of the federal Gateway National Recreation Area after it was decommissioned and today boasts sports fields, park land and a recreation center. Davis, the driver, and a 45-year-old woman in the front passenger seat were all rushed to Brookdale University Hospital, where Davis died. Davis lived in Bushwick, according to cops. The other two women are expected to recover. The building they crashed was left with extensive structural damage, officials said. One of the women remained pinned for some time in the car as first responders tried to get her out, according to early reports. The NYPD’s Collision Investigation Squad is investigating what sparked the crash.

articles.title: Inside the mind of a serial killer: What experts say about Gilgo Beach killer Rex Heuermann
articles.article_text: --- title: Inside the mind of a serial killer: What experts say about Gilgo Beach killer Rex Heuermann url: https://www.fox5ny.com/video/fmc-od9qw6mwiuf47638 hostname: fox5ny.com description: What drives a serial killer — and how did Rex Heuermann go undetected for so long? After pleading guilty to multiple murders tied to the Gilgo Beach case, Heuermann is now expected to speak with the FBI’s Behavioral Analysis Unit. Experts say the interviews could offer rare insight into how serial killers think, operate and evade detection. Psychologists and investigators explain how Heuermann allegedly lived a double life, concealed his crimes for decades and may have kept mementos from victims. They also discuss what the FBI hopes to learn — and how it could help solve future crimes. Sentencing is scheduled for June 17. sitename: FOX 5 New York date: 2026-04-09 --- Inside the mind of a serial killer: What experts say about Gilgo Beach killer Rex Heuermann What drives a serial killer — and how did Rex Heuermann go undetected for so long? After pleading guilty to multiple murders tied to the Gilgo Beach case, Heuermann is now expected to speak with the FBI’s Behavioral Analysis Unit. Experts say the interviews could offer rare insight into how serial killers think, operate and evade detection. Psychologists and investigators explain how Heuermann allegedly lived a double life, concealed his crimes for decades and may have kept mementos from victims. They also discuss what the FBI hopes to learn — and how it could help solve future crimes. Sentencing is scheduled for June 17.

Along with breaking news and weather, “The Rush Hour” features NY1’s signature coverage of politics, mass transit, the school system, Broadway and more. Annika utilizes her decades of reporting on New York to give the show an additional focus on the power players who move this city — from businesses to nonprofits to major institutions. Watch “The Rush Hour” weekdays at 4 p.m. The show airs on NY1 and is available to Spectrum subscribers on Channel 1 and via the Spectrum News App for mobile, Xumo Stream Box, Roku and Apple TV streaming devices. Annika Pergament is an Emmy Award-winning journalist with 30 years of experience covering New York’s biggest stories for NY1, including the terror attacks on Sept. 11, the financial crisis of 2007-2008 and the COVID-19 pandemic. Her recent pieces include an investigation into New York City’s Administration for Children’s Services, an interview with Alex Bolen, CEO of Oscar de la Renta, and a profile of the director and actor Jennifer Esposito.

articles.title: Coca-Cola unveils limited-edition America250 cans for NY and NJ
articles.author: Amanda Hurley
articles.article_text: --- title: Coca-Cola unveils limited-edition America250 cans for NY and NJ author: Amanda Hurley url: https://www.fox5ny.com/news/coca-cola-unveils-limited-edition-america250-cans-ny-nj hostname: fox5ny.com description: Coca-Cola is releasing a limited edition lineup of state-specific cans to celebrate America's 250th birthday. sitename: FOX 5 New York date: 2026-04-08 tags: ['Us,Ny,Nyc,News,Us,Nj,America 250'] --- NEW YORK CITY - Coca-Cola is adding a little extra pop to this year's Semiquincentennial celebrations! What we know: The beverage company is releasing a limited edition lineup of state-specific cans to celebrate America's 250th birthday. Each mini-can will feature a design unique to all 50 states, as well as Puerto Rico and the District of Columbia. The Empire State Building appears on the New York can, while a lighthouse shows up on New Jersey's can. The America250 artwork will appear on regular Coke, Coke Zero and Diet Coke mini-cans, according to USA Today. Coca-Cola bottles will also feature limited-edition America250 packaging. What's next: The limited-edition cans will be available at major retailers, including Walmart and Target, nationwide this month. What you can do: This new line of America250 cans presents a brand-new opportunity for collectors to find all 52 cans! You can also scan all participating products to unlock prizes and rewards, including the chance to win a new Jeep. The Source: Information from this article was sourced from Coca-Cola and USA Today.

articles.article_text: --- title: State Sen. Krueger Suffers Stroke url: https://www.ourtownny.com/news/state-sen-krueger-suffers-stroke-JE5745685 hostname: ourtownny.com description: State Sen. Liz Krueger suffered a mild stroke late last week and is âup and about and doing well, and a full recovery is expected,â according to Justin Flagg, t... sitename: www.ourtownny.com date: 2026-04-09 tags: ['health', 'liz krueger', 'stroke', 'heart health'] --- State Sen. Krueger Suffers Stroke She is doing well, and âa full recovery is expected,â according to her staff. State Sen. Liz Krueger suffered a mild stroke late last week and is âup and about and doing well, and a full recovery is expected,â according to Justin Flagg, the senatorâs director of communications. Krueger, a Democrat who represents District 28 in Manhattan, âis currently recuperating at home, and is in regular contact with Senate leadership and staff while she takes time to rest and recover. She and her family are greatly appreciative of the responsiveness of the staff at Lenox Hill Hospital,â Flagg said in a statement to Our Town. Krueger was elected to the New York State Senate in a special election in February 2002. She is currently the chair of the Senate Finance Committee and is a member of the Budget and Revenue and Rules Committees. Kruegerâs virtual town hall on election security, set for Monday, April 13, from 6:30 p.m. â 7:45 p.m., will take place as planned, but the senator will no longer be participating.

articles.title: Hip-hop pioneer, Afrika Bambaataa, dies at 67: Reports
articles.author: Catherine Stoddard
articles.article_text: --- title: Hip-hop pioneer, Afrika Bambaataa, dies at 67: Reports author: Catherine Stoddard url: https://www.fox5ny.com/news/hip-hop-pioneer-afrika-bambaataadies-67-reports hostname: fox5ny.com description: Rapper and DJ, Afrika Bambaataa, has died, according to multiple reports. He was 67 years old. sitename: FOX 5 New York date: 2026-04-09 tags: ['Entertainment,News'] --- Rapper and DJ, Afrika Bambaataa, has died, according to multiple reports. He was 67 years old. Bambaataa, whose real name is Lance Taylor, died on Thursday due to complications from cancer, according to TMZ. Dig deeper: Bambaataa’s notable contributions included a series of electro tracks he released in the 1980s that influenced the development of hip hop culture. He was also one of the founders of the hip-hop awareness group, Universal Zulu Nation, according to Complex. Bambaataa controversy The backstory: In 2021, Bambaataa was sued over allegations of child sexual abuse, according to The Guardian. An anonymous man alleged in a lawsuit filed in New York, that Bambaataa abused him for four years starting at the age of 12. The abuse began in the early 1990s. Bambaataa ended up losing the civil case because he failed to enter a legal response to the lawsuit, Complex reported. The Source: Information for this article was taken from reporting by TMZ, Newsweek, and Complex, and previous reporting by The Guardian. This story was reported from San Jose.

articles.title: Mamdani announces New York City's 2-K will expand to all day, year-round program
articles.article_text: --- title: Mamdani announces New York City's 2-K will expand to all day, year-round program url: https://abc7ny.com/post/mamdani-expands-nycs-2-program-accommodate-working-parents-full-day/18861285/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: Mayor Mamdani said the program, as originally planned, was too short and failed to meet the needs of working parents. sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2026-04-09 categories: ['education'] tags: ['18861285', 'education,new-york-city-schools,zohran-mamdani,New-York-City'] --- NEW YORK (WABC) -- Mayor Zohran Mamdani announced on Thursday morning that the city's 2-K program will expand to run for a full day and it will be year-round. Free child care for 2-year-olds begins this fall. The program will serve a limited number of children with 2,000 seats. Mamdani said the program, as originally planned, was too short and failed to meet the needs of working parents. The program will now last from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. "That is gonna be 10 hours a day, 260 days a year, a typical school year, as we know, only lasts 180 days," Mamdani said. "What this will do is save working parents at least $20,000 per child." There are plans to expand 2-K to 12,000 seats by 2027 in all five boroughs. State funding is helping to make the program possible. ---------- Have a breaking news tip or an idea for a story we should cover? Send it to Eyewitness News using the form below. If attaching a video or photo, terms of use apply.

Please be sure to leave contact information in case we have follow-up questions. Your contact information and identity will not be shared beyond our team or used in our reporting. A great investigative news tip should explain the situation in detail, be newsworthy, and have real-world consequences. Please be very specific about the problem and include any documents or evidence you might have. While we understand you may want to be anonymous, we still may need to speak with someone to get more information and help point us in the right direction. We appreciate you sharing your investigative story idea. Due to the high volume of tips, we only respond to tips we are interested in pursuing.

articles.title: Bronx rapper and DJ Afrika Bambaataa dies at 67
articles.author: Brian Niemietz
articles.article_text: --- title: Bronx rapper and DJ Afrika Bambaataa dies at 67 author: Brian Niemietz url: https://www.nydailynews.com/2026/04/09/afrika-bambaataa-dead-67-cancer/ hostname: nydailynews.com description: Rapper Afrika Bambaataa is dead at 67. The Bronx native reportedly died from complications connected to cancer around 3 a.m. in Pennsylvania. sitename: New York Daily News date: 2026-04-09 categories: ['National News'] tags: ['Afrika Bambaataa, dead, cancer, 67, Bronx, hip hop, rapper, DJ'] --- Pioneering rapper and DJ Afrika Bambaataa has died. The Bronx native was 67. The Hip Hop Alliance, headed by fellow hip hop icon Kurtis Blow, announced his death Thursday. “Today, we acknowledge the transition of a foundational architect of Hip Hop culture, Afrika Bambaataa,” the message said. “As the founder of the Universal Zulu Nation, Afrika Bambaataa helped shape the early identity of Hip Hop as a global movement rooted in peace, unity, love, and having fun.” Blow acknowledged the performer, born Lance Taylor, leaves behind a legacy that is “complex,” likely an allusion to the multiple allegations of child abuse against the musician, dating as far back as the 1970s. TMZ said Bambaataa died of complications from cancer around 3 a.m. in Pennsylvania. This is a developing story.

articles.title: Local Event: Death in New York: Extended Daylight Tour (Lower Manhattan)
articles.author: Purefinder New York; Neighbor
articles.article_text: --- title: Local Event: Death in New York: Extended Daylight Tour (Lower Manhattan) author: Purefinder New York; Neighbor url: https://patch.com/new-york/upper-east-side-nyc/calendar/event/20260411/8c4d8033-069c-4f44-a766-fbbfb50d63b9/death-in-new-york-extended-daylight-tour-lower-manhattan hostname: patch.com description: Check out the latest community post from one of your neighbors. (The views expressed in this post are the author’s own.) sitename: Upper East Side, NY Patch date: 2026-04-01 categories: ['Calendar'] --- The Death in New York: Extended Daylight Tour returns for a limited spring run. New York City’s past is filled with stories that reveal how people lived with death long before modern medicine and reform. This extended walking tour examines the city’s history of burial grounds, epidemics, and public executions across Lower Manhattan. Participants visit sites in Battery Park, the Financial District, Tribeca, the Civic Center, and Chinatown to learn how disease, faith, and policy shaped the city’s development. From colonial churchyards to early cemeteries and prison sites, the tour offers a detailed look at how New York’s attitudes toward mortality helped define its growth. Voted among Time Out New York's Top 10!

articles.article_text: --- title: Video shows Pope Leo meeting the Harlem Globetrotters, spin basketball on his finger author: Jesse Zanger url: https://www.cbsnews.com/newyork/news/pope-leo-meets-the-harlem-globetrotters/ hostname: cbsnews.com description: Pope Leo enjoyed a lighthearted moment Wednesday as he welcomed the Harlem Globetrotters to St. Peter's Square. sitename: CBS New York date: 2026-04-09 categories: ['Local News'] tags: ['Harlem Globetrotters, Pope Leo XIV'] --- Pope Leo enjoyed a lighthearted moment Wednesday as he welcomed the Harlem Globetrotters to St. Peter's Square. During the audience, Pope Leo was seen attempting one of the team's classic tricks - spinning their red, white and blue basketball on a finger, with help from one of the players. The moment drew cheers and smiles from those present. The pontiff then shook hands with the players, and held up a customized Harlem Globetrotters jersey with "Pope Leo XIV" emblazoned on it. The Harlem Globetrotters have entertained fans worldwide since they got started in 1926. Back in 2021, they wrote an open letter to NBA Commissioner Adam Silver .
